---
title: "tweedie"
generated: true
description: "A Tweedie family with **fixed** power `p` (compound Poisson-Gamma)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.glm`](../nitrix-stats-glm.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:tweedie -->
```python
tweedie(p: float = 1.5) -> Family
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:tweedie -->
A Tweedie family with **fixed** power `p` (compound Poisson-Gamma).

The regime $1 < p < 2$ is the semicontinuous one (an exact zero with
probability mass plus a continuous positive part: rainfall, insurance claims,
some imaging measures).  Variance $V(\mu) = \mu^p$, log link,
dispersion estimated (`has_fixed_dispersion=False`, like Gamma).  With
`p` fixed the *mean* coefficients fit the shared IRLS core directly
(working weight $\mu^{2-p}$); jointly profiling `p` is a follow-up
($p \to 1$ recovers Poisson, $p \to 2$ Gamma).

The unit deviance is the closed form

$d(y, \mu) = 2\left[ y^{2-p}/((1-p)(2-p)) - y\,\mu^{1-p}/(1-p) + \mu^{2-p}/(2-p) \right]$;

the log-likelihood uses the **saddlepoint approximation** (with the exact
compound-Poisson zero mass at $y = 0$) -- enough for [`aic`](../nitrix-stats-glm/aic.qmd) /
[`bic`](../nitrix-stats-glm/bic.qmd) model comparison; the exact Dunn-Smyth series is a follow-up.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:tweedie -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `p` | `float` | — | `1.5` | The fixed Tweedie power $p$, entering the variance as $\mu^p$. Must satisfy $1 < p < 2$ (the compound Poisson-Gamma regime). |

**Returns** `Family`. A Tweedie family with the given fixed power, a log link, and an estimated dispersion.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
